citations.description: Ticket comments contain only: 'FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified.' No dispatch run URL, run ID, or confirmation of the workflow_dispatch trigger is recorded.

findings.detail: This is an operational deployment step (triggering a GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch) that has no codebase representation and leaves no verifiable artifact in the repository. The ticket provides no attestation of this dispatch having occurred — the only comment is the FORCE_CLAIM note, which contains no reference ID, timestamp of dispatch, run URL, or observed outcome. The criterion is untagged, so it defaults to codebase-verifiable, but no codebase evidence can confirm a live workflow was dispatched.
findings.remediation: Record a ticket comment with the specific GitHub Actions run URL (e.g. https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/<run-id>), the observed outcome (successful publish to PyPI, tag created), and the date/time of the dispatch with version=0.9.0.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
